description: Represents throughput of AI model inference, measured as tokens per second (TPS), the number of output tokens a model can generate every second. It’s a key indicator of model speed, responsiveness, and scalability for both user-facing and agentic (multi-step reasoning or tool-using) tasks.Speeds have increased exponentially, from ~15 TPS in 2021 (e.g., GPT-3 on A100 GPUs) to over 1,000 TPS in 2025 (e.g., Llama 4 Maverick on Blackwell/Cerebras). This ~67x improvement is driven by hardware (e.g., Blackwell FP4 support), optimizations (e.g., speculative decoding, KV caching), and architectural advances (e.g., MoE in Llama 4). Early models were serial and memory-bound; modern ones achieve real-time (human-reading-speed ~10–20 TPS) or superhuman rates for agentic tasks.
